description: Regression prompts for flagship AERS skills. These are not model-specific scores; they define expected artifacts and review checks for repeatable human or agent evaluation.

evals.title: StatsPAI Card IV Pipeline
evals.prompt: Using the Card returns-to-schooling example, run an end-to-end IV empirical pipeline with StatsPAI-style APIs. Produce a data contract, sample construction note, first-stage diagnostics, weak-IV assessment, main 2SLS estimate, robustness table, and a short interpretation suitable for an applied economics appendix.
checks: States the endogenous regressor, instrument, outcome, and identifying variation before estimating.
Reports first-stage strength and weak-IV risk rather than only second-stage coefficients.
Separates descriptive sample facts from causal interpretation.
Flags limitations of the instrument and external validity.
failure_modes: Treats OLS and IV estimates as interchangeable.
Omits first-stage or weak-IV diagnostics.
Claims causal identification without stating exclusion or relevance assumptions.

evals.title: Python LaLonde Observational Pipeline
evals.prompt: Build a transparent Python pipeline for the LaLonde training-data example using pandas, statsmodels or linearmodels, and common plotting libraries. Include cleaning, Table 1 balance, propensity-score overlap diagnostics, ATT estimates under at least two specifications, and a robustness appendix.
checks: Uses explicit library calls instead of hiding the workflow behind an unexplained DSL.
Reports covariate balance before and after adjustment.
Explains estimand differences across OLS, matching, weighting, or doubly robust specifications.
Keeps generated tables reproducible from the script.
failure_modes: Uses treatment prediction accuracy as the causal estimand.
Ignores overlap and balance diagnostics.
Mixes train/test language into an observational causal design without justification.

evals.title: Stata LaLonde Replication .do File
evals.prompt: Write a Stata .do replication workflow for the LaLonde training-data example. The file should install or check required community packages, import data, generate balance tables, run baseline and adjusted treatment-effect specifications, export publication-style tables, and create a concise log of assumptions and diagnostics.
checks: Includes deterministic setup, package checks, paths, and logging.
Uses Stata idioms for balance, fixed effects, robust inference, and exports.
Documents which estimand each command is targeting.
Keeps output paths separate from source data.
failure_modes: Writes pseudo-Stata that would not run.
Omits log capture or reproducibility setup.
Exports only screenshots or manual tables.

evals.title: R LaLonde Quarto Report
evals.prompt: Create an R + Quarto empirical report for the LaLonde training-data example. Use tidyverse-style cleaning, MatchIt or WeightIt diagnostics, fixest or equivalent estimators, modelsummary tables, and publication-ready figures. The report should render to HTML and include a reproducibility appendix.
checks: Uses Quarto structure for narrative, code, tables, figures, and appendix.
Shows balance and overlap before presenting treatment effects.
Reports package versions or session information.
Keeps code chunks reproducible and non-interactive.
failure_modes: Produces prose without runnable R chunks.
Omits balance diagnostics.
Relies on manual table editing outside the report.

evals.title: AER Staggered DID Identification Audit
evals.prompt: Audit a draft paper that estimates a staggered-adoption difference-in-differences model with heterogeneous treatment effects and only a short pre-period. Produce an estimand-first diagnosis, threat matrix, required diagnostics, preferred modern DID estimators, and referee-facing language for why the design is or is not credible.
checks: Separates the target estimand from the estimator.
Flags TWFE contamination risks under staggered adoption and heterogeneous effects.
Specifies concrete diagnostics rather than generic robustness claims.
States what evidence would make the design non-credible.
failure_modes: Recommends TWFE as sufficient without qualification.
Treats event-study pre-trends as definitive proof.
Adds robustness checks without addressing the estimand.

evals.title: AER Submission Preflight
evals.prompt: Run a pre-submission audit for an AER or AEJ empirical paper. Check abstract length, contribution framing, disclosure statements, data and code availability, replication package readiness, cover letter fit, and likely desk-rejection risks. Return a prioritized action list.
checks: Uses concrete AER or AEJ constraints instead of generic journal advice.
Checks replication and disclosure obligations explicitly.
Prioritizes blocking issues above polish.
Keeps the recommendation tied to the manuscript's contribution and design.
failure_modes: Gives generic style edits while missing submission constraints.
Ignores data availability and author disclosure requirements.
Fails to distinguish AER from AEJ or AER: Insights routing.

evals.title: AER Replication Package Audit
evals.prompt: Audit a replication package for an accepted applied economics paper before AEA Data Editor review. Check README completeness, master script execution order, environment capture, raw versus derived data separation, restricted-data handling, random seeds, expected runtime, and table/figure reproduction mapping.
checks: Maps each paper table and figure to exact scripts and output files.
Distinguishes public, restricted, raw, intermediate, and final artifacts.
Captures runtime, software versions, seeds, and operating-system assumptions.
Flags any steps that require human judgment or unavailable data.
failure_modes: Treats a code dump as a replication package.
Omits data-use restrictions or access instructions.
Does not define a single master execution path.

evals.title: Chinese Academic De-AIGC Rewrite
evals.prompt: Rewrite a Chinese empirical-paper abstract and introduction paragraph to reduce formulaic AI-writing signals while preserving claims, variable definitions, identifying assumptions, and empirical results. Provide a before/after diagnostic table and a short note on what was changed.
checks: Preserves empirical claims, coefficients, sample, and caveats.
Reduces repetitive transitions, slogan-like phrasing, and symmetric paragraph rhythm.
Keeps an academic register rather than converting to casual prose.
Separates stylistic edits from substantive changes.
failure_modes: Changes empirical claims or identification assumptions.
Over-humanizes into colloquial or promotional writing.
Only swaps synonyms while leaving the same sentence rhythm.
